description_long: This is a short, broken wooden fence constructed from rough, dark brown planks. The fence features two horizontal rails, each fastened with visible metal bolts, and several vertical pickets, some of which are jagged, splintered, or missing, giving it a damaged and weathered appearance. The wood grain is clearly visible, and the planks are unevenly cut, with some ends pointed and others blunt or fractured. The structure is irregular, with one picket leaning outward and another lying detached, suggesting neglect or recent impact. The overall look is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for temporary or makeshift barriers in urban parks, alleys, or construction zones. There are no decorative elements or post caps, emphasizing its functional, improvised nature.
description: A damaged, rustic wooden fence with jagged, uneven planks and visible metal bolts, showing signs of breakage and weathering. The structure is irregular and utilitarian, lacking decorative features.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broken and jagged planks most clearly, with one detached piece and visible metal bolts on the horizontal rails.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thin profile of the fence, emphasizing the flatness of the rails and the protrusion of the vertical pickets.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the same broken and uneven planks, with the detached piece more visible and the wood grain prominent.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the thinness of the structure and the irregular alignment of the planks.
